task_completion.description: Verify that the MRHead sample data is loaded in Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data module or Subject Hierarchy shows 'MRHead', or the slice views display the standard brain MRI anatomy.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Angle markup tool has been selected or an Angle node created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module shows an 'Angle' node in the list, or the Angle icon is active in the toolbar.

task_completion.description: Verify that exactly three control points are placed to define the angle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Red (axial) slice view or 3D view, three distinct control points are visible, connected by lines forming an angle.

task_completion.description: Verify that the points are placed on the lateral ventricles in the axial view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red slice view shows the 'butterfly' shape of the lateral ventricles (approx slice 125-140), and the markup points are positioned on/near these structures.

task_completion.description: Verify that the angle measurement value is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric angle value (in degrees) is visible next to the middle control point in the viewer or listed in the Markups module measurement table.

task_completion.description: Verify that the measured angle was saved to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing a text editor or terminal displaying the content of 'angle_measurement.txt', which must match the numeric value seen in the Slicer interface.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Slicer GUI to place points rather than scripting the result without visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Slicer application window with the Markups interface or toolbar active during the process.

integrity.description: Verify that the exported value matches the visual measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: The number in the text file must correspond to the angle displayed by the markup tool in the Slicer viewport.

integrity.description: Verify that the result is derived from the actual placement of points.
integrity.visual_evidence: The angle lines in the viewer must geometrically correspond to the reported value (e.g., a 90-degree visual angle should not result in a 10-degree output).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the 'MRHead.nrrd' sample dataset. The 3D Slicer Angle tool requires exactly 3 control points to define an angle. The angle is measured at the second (middle) point.
